title: Calibration Protocol for OD600 to Colony Forming Units (CFUs)
author: Richard Tennant
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zgnf3ve
materials: You have now completed this calibration protocol

steps.title: This protocol will result in CFU/mL for 0
steps.instruction: This protocol will result in CFU/mL for 0.1 OD 600 . Your overnight cultures will have a much higher OD 600 and so this section of the protocol, called “Sample Preparation”, will give you the “Starting Sample” with a 0.1 OD 600 measurement.

steps.title: Measure the OD 600 of your...
steps.instruction: Measure the OD 600 of your cell cultures, making sure to dilute to the linear detection range of your plate reader. e.g. Add 25 μL culture to 175 μL LB + Chloramphenicol (Cam) in a well in a black 96-well plate, with a clear, flat bottom

steps.title: Recommended plate setup is below
steps.instruction: Recommended plate setup is below. Each well should have 200 μL

steps.title: Dilute your overnight culture to OD 600 = 0
steps.instruction: Dilute your overnight culture to OD 600 = 0.1 in 1mL of LB + Cam media. Do this in triplicate for each culture. Use (C 1 )(V 1 ) = (C 2 )(V 2 ) to calculate your dilutions C 1 is your starting OD 600 C 2 is your target OD 600 (= 0.1) V 1 is the unknown volume in μL V 2 is the final volume (= 1000 μL) Important: When calculating C 1 , subtract the blank from your reading and multiple by the dilution factor you used. Example: C 1 = (1:8 OD 600 - blank OD 600 ) x 8 = (0.195 - 0.042) x 8 = 0.153 x 8 = 1.224 Example: (C 1 )(V 1 ) = (C 2 )(V 2 ) (1.224)(x) = (0.1)(1000 μL) x = 100/1.224 = 82 μL culture Add 82 μL of culture to 918 μL media for a total volume of 1000 μL

steps.title: Check the OD 600 and make sure it is 0
steps.instruction: Check the OD 600 and make sure it is 0.1. Recommended plate setup is below. Each well should have 200 μL.

steps.title: Do the following serial dilutions for...
steps.instruction: Do the following serial dilutions for your triplicate Starting Samples you prepared in Step 5. You should have 12 total Starting Samples - 6 for your Positive Controls and 6 for your Negative Controls.

steps.title: You will need 3 LB Agar...
steps.instruction: You will need 3 LB Agar + Cam plates (36 total)

steps.title: Prepare three 2
steps.instruction: Prepare three 2.0 mL tubes (36 total) with 1900 μL of LB + Cam media for Dilutions 1, 2, and 3

steps.title: Prepare two 1
steps.instruction: Prepare two 1.5 mL tubes (24 total) with 900 μL of LB + Cam media for Dilutions 4 and 5

steps.title: Label each tube according to the...
steps.instruction: Label each tube according to the figure above (Dilution 1, etc.) for each Starting Sample

steps.title: Pipet 100 μL of Starting Culture into Dilution 1
steps.instruction: Pipet 100 μL of Starting Culture into Dilution 1. Discard tip. Do NOT pipette up and down. Vortex tube for 5-10 secs

steps.title: Repeat Step 11 for each dilution...
steps.instruction: Repeat Step 11 for each dilution through to Dilution 5 as shown above

steps.title: Aseptically spead plate 100 μL on...
steps.instruction: Aseptically spead plate 100 μL on LB + Cam plates for Dilutions 3, 4, and 5

steps.title: Incubate at 37 °C overnight and...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 37 °C overnight and count colonies after 18-20 hours of growth

steps.title: Based on the assumption that 1...
steps.instruction: Based on the assumption that 1 bacterial cell gives rise to 1 colony, colony forming units (CFU) per 1mL of an OD 600 = 0.1 culture can be calculated

steps.title: First, count the colonies on each...
steps.instruction: First, count the colonies on each plate with fewer than 300 colonies

steps.title: Next, multiply the colony count by...
steps.instruction: Next, multiply the colony count by the Final Dilution Factor on each plate Example using Dilution 4 from above: # colonies x Final Dilution Factor = CFU/mL 125 x (8 x 10 5 ) = 1 x 10 8 CFU ⁄ mL in Starting Sample (OD 600 = 0.1)
